architecture_summary: PaLI is a multimodal sequence-to-sequence Transformer derived from the T5 encoder-decoder architecture that takes an image and text as input and autoregressively generates text. It reuses pre-trained mT5 language backbones and ViT vision backbones, and scales both modalities jointly with a large multilingual image-text pretraining mix.

novel_contributions: We introduce PaLI, a jointly scaled multilingual language-image model with a single image-and-text to text interface.
We create WebLI, a new high-volume multilingual image-and-language dataset containing 10 billion images and texts in over 100 languages.
We train ViT-e, a 4-billion parameter ViT, to study scaling of the vision backbone beyond previous models.
We show that jointly scaling the vision and language components improves performance, with the vision side giving a better return on investment.
We empirically validate that a mixture-of-objectives benefits large vision-and-language models.
We show that a properly-scaled model can handle many languages while still achieving SOTA performance on English-only tasks.

key_claims.claim: PaLI uses a simple, modular image-and-text to text interface that can handle image-only, language-only, and multimodal tasks across many languages.
Joint scaling of the vision and language components is important, and the vision backbone provides a better return on investment than the language backbone.
PaLI-17B achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks, including COCO Captioning and VQAv2.
WebLI is a new multilingual image-text dataset with 10 billion images and texts in over 100 languages.
ViT-e is a 4B-parameter vision transformer and improves PaLI’s vision-language performance compared with ViT-G.
PaLI-17B preserves strong language-understanding capabilities despite multimodal pretraining.
The mixture-of-objectives design is effective, with split-captioning, object-related tasks, and OCR contributing strongly.
